result:
**Goal for Round 41: Cell Probe Inspector**

Add a cell probe tool (toggled with `?` key) that lets the user click any cell to display a detail panel showing **all 14+ analysis metrics at that position simultaneously** — entropy, temperature, Lyapunov exponent, Fourier power, fractal dimension, surprisal, mutual information, Kolmogorov complexity, etc. — along with the cell's age, species, zone rule, and neighborhood configuration.

**Why this exploits existing work perfectly:** The project has 14 independent scientific overlays, each computing per-cell values into dedicated grids (`entropy_grid`, `lyapunov_grid`, `kc_grid`, etc.), but there's currently no way to see all metrics for a single cell at once. Users must toggle overlays one at a time to compare. This feature is pure UI integration over existing computation — zero new algorithms, maximum leverage of 15 rounds of analysis infrastructure. It transforms the overlays from isolated views into a unified diagnostic instrument.

Shall I build it?
